title: Drawing a Landscape
description: Build a picture by combining shapes. Focus: coordinates, layering order (what gets drawn on top), and reading shape parameters.

flashcards.definition: Shapes drawn later appear on top of shapes drawn earlier.
flashcards.example:
Circle(200, 200, 120)
Rect(0, 300, 400, 100)  # this rectangle will cover the bottom of the circle

flashcards.definition: A landscape scene is often built from simple shapes: sky, ground, sun, clouds, mountains, trees.
flashcards.example:
# sky (background)
Rect(0, 0, 400, 400)
# ground
Rect(0, 280, 400, 120)
# sun
Circle(320, 80, 40)

flashcards.definition: Draws a rectangle. left/top is the top-left corner. width/height control size.
(0,0) is top-left. x increases right. y increases down.
flashcards.example:
# sky is near y=0
# ground is near y=400

quiz.prompt: If two shapes overlap, which one appears on top?
options: The shape drawn later (lower in the code)
The shape with the larger x value
quiz.feedbackCorrect: Yes: draw order matters. Later shapes appear on top.
quiz.feedbackIncorrect: Draw order matters: shapes drawn later appear on top.

quiz.prompt: In Rect(0, 280, 400, 120), what do 400 and 120 represent?
quiz.feedbackCorrect: Correct: Rect uses (left, top, width, height).

quiz.prompt: If you want the ground at the bottom of the 400×400 canvas, which y value should it be near?
quiz.feedbackCorrect: Correct: bigger y is lower (toward the bottom).
quiz.feedbackIncorrect: Bigger y is lower on the canvas, so bottom is near y=400.
